cache layer returns structured decision records; per-input hashes in v2 entries name the changed input; cache-hit? kept as thin wrapper; 89 tests pass
--explain prints per-task reason with named inputs/outputs; conditional skips name the upstream that causes them; pure walk-explanations unit-tested; 99 tests pass

staleness as Datalog rules over decision facts; blames/2 gives the transitive chain; --why CLI renders it as a tree; cross-checked against walk-explanations on synthetic + beeatlas graphs; 141 tests pass
run-plan records per-task decision+outcome; trace.rkt persists them versioned under .stelis/; --explain --last replays without re-fingerprinting; graceful degradation tested; 151 tests pass
all four slices landed: decision records, --explain, Datalog why-stale chains, persisted build trace

Cutoff is emergent from input-addressing (run-plan re-decides each task after upstreams ran); implemented the receipt: cache entry v3 stores per-output content hashes (derived+resolvable only, per the derived-vs-authoritative rule), post-run comparison yields an output-delta ('identical = the cutoff point) recorded on trace v3, printed during --build and rendered by --explain --last. Snapshot-less tasks (boundary, dbt) now store outputs-only entries so their reruns have a comparison basis. End-to-end acceptance test in src/exec-test.rkt; ADR 0003.
Row-coherent db-relation content-addressing shipped and verified; per-column substrate split to st-7vz

species.json built+verified E2E: byte-identical (4b987c55) to production public/data/species.json AND to a direct uv invocation; second run cache-skips; --why/--explain --last report it; fixed missing occurrences.parquet input on species-export; regression test added. All 191 tests pass.

place-marts node placing 6 dbt marts to EXPORT_DIR shipped; collectors.json built+verified byte-identical to a direct invocation; place-marts/species-export/collectors-export all cache-skip on rerun; fixed geojson resolution gap (surfaced by place-marts never caching) and collectors-export's sandbox-vs-@export input fidelity. occurrences.db plan unchanged (place-marts pruned). 199 tests pass.

Guard added: check-file-outputs-resolvable (cache.rkt) rejects any plan whose 'file output resolves to #f, wired into --build and --verify (main.rkt). feeds now errors loudly; shipped targets pass. plan-test #11 covers both cases.

Built edge-verify.rkt harness: runs a task in a fresh EXPORT_DIR seeded with ONLY its declared @export inputs, asserts it still succeeds (inputs sufficient) and wrote exactly its declared outputs (outputs complete). Fixed-path inputs (sandbox/DB/seeds) are ambient. Pure classify-outputs + export-dir-artifact? unit-tested (edge-verify-test.rkt); gated integration test retro-validates the 4 shipped terminals (edge-verify-integration-test.rkt, skips w/o a reference build). Retro-validation FOUND a real slice-1 gap: species-export writes 6 files, edge declared 2 — added seasonality/photos/species_hosts/higher_taxa.json outputs+artifacts+paths; now 4/4 clean. No CLI (per decision). 224 tests pass.

Refactored beeatlas.rkt placement to single-source lists: sandbox-marts (9 dbt outputs), placed-marts (6 copied), export-dir-files (terminal single-file exports), raw-file-paths. Derived from them: the 9 mart 'file artifacts, dbt-build #:outputs, mart-export-artifacts, place-marts inputs/outputs, place-marts-script, and beeatlas-path resolution. Collapsed beeatlas-path/case (sandbox case-list + terminal enum) into one cond over the lists. Removed the mart-files duplication. Directory outputs (species-maps/place-maps/feeds) deliberately kept unresolvable (#f) so the st-6qc guard still refuses them — placement-as-property did NOT auto-derive export-dir for all file outputs (that would defeat st-6qc and mismodel dir outputs). Behavior-preserving: 224 tests pass, edge-verify 4/4 clean, places.json/occurrences.db plans resolve unchanged.

Stelis-side timestamp policy shipped: ADR 0004 + SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH injection into every task + determinism harness support + test. Concrete beeatlas built_at fix carried by beeatlas-8td SITE 1 (contract recorded there).

--verify now works for post-dbt terminals. (1) out-file derived from the target artifact's on-disk name via beeatlas-path (was hardcoded occurrences.db); (2) a --from suffix's fresh build dirs are seeded with the suffix's EXTERNAL @export inputs (verify-seeds in main.rkt) from the populated scratch dir, so the suffix builds without the upstream cone. determinism.rkt gained #:seed + #:out-file wiring. Demonstrated DETERMINISTIC on counties/ecoregions.clean.geojson, collectors.events.json, and the 16MB collector_event_pages.json sidecar. Bonus: the st-6qc guard (run by --verify) surfaced a latent slice-4 gap — collector_event_pages.json was missing from export-dir-files (unresolvable); fixed, and plan-test now guards both terminals' full output resolvability. 245 tests green.

'dir artifact kind + order-independent tree digest (tree-digest.rkt); species-maps/place-maps/feeds now buildable, content-addressed, cacheable. Determinism verified on a real 591-file species-maps build. Slice under open epic st-4cm.

Declared fan-out key: 'dir artifacts gain #:keyed-by (list of fan-out branches: input, key-column, filename-template). New fan-out-key.rkt verifies files ⊆ input keys (soundness GATED) and reports keys-without-files (completeness). Wired place-maps (keyed by places.json[].slug); validated on real build: 132/181 sound, 49 filtered reported. Multi-branch supported+unit-tested for species-maps' shape. species-maps/feeds stay opaque 'dir until wired (species-maps needs species.parquet declared — follow-on). Slice under open epic st-4cm.

Directory terminals (species-maps/place-maps/feeds) finished: edges corrected to real reads, all determinism-gated, place-maps fan-out-verified. Coverage broadened; follow-ons st-5jt + st-msn filed. Two beeatlas determinism fixes (SITE 3 + feeds SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH) uncommitted.

notes.json relabeled 'derived (was mislabeled 'authoritative); the authoritative label moved to the INPUT notes store, modeled as a producerless 'file leaf at NOTES_DB_PATH (forward-only by construction, content-hashed when present). Fixed notes-harvest's under-declared edge (the store was missing). notes.json is now cutoff-eligible — the hook the CRUD→targeted-rebuild goal (st-066) needs. 287 tests. No beeatlas change; store not mounted locally so runtime gating N/A.
species-maps fan-out wired + verified (785/785 sound). Composite keys, parquet key source, tuple core delivered; duckdb.rkt extracted and shared. Unblocks st-q6i (feeds manifest).

feeds verified via its emitted manifest (feeds/index.json) — NO beeatlas change needed: index.json already maps filename→verbatim filter_value + filter_type. New manifest-key declaration + verify-manifest: checks {files}=={manifest entries}∪{singletons} AND each entry's filter_value is a real DISTINCT value of its ecdysis_data source column (via duckdb). 232/232 sound, 0 incomplete (feeds writes one per distinct collector/genus, unfiltered). Consolidated SQL-safety patterns into duckdb.rkt. All 3 dir terminals now set-verified (place-maps/species-maps/feeds). 292 tests.
First slice of st-sds implemented and tested end-to-end

Per-key observations shipped: 'dir outputs record per-file (path->hash) timelines; --history <dir> attributes changes to specific keys; observed-key/4 Datalog projection. Keyed by relative path (domain-key decode deferred to H2). 327 tests pass.

Per-column relation digests shipped, scoped up to relations-as-observed-outputs. relation-columns emits per-column multiset digest + non-null count (separate query; combined row-coherent digest untouched = still the skip signal). db-relation is now the third observed artifact kind: output-snapshot+keys observes it via resolve-relation (+ new resolve-relation-columns build-env slot); per-column rides the SAME output-key-hashes/history/observed-key machinery as per-key. --history <relation> shows which columns changed per build. 334 tests pass. Domain-key/attribute-lineage modeling still deferred to H2.

Integrity gate shipped, done the node way. New in-process rule-check modality in exec.rkt (a rule evaluated in Racket as a graph node, gating downstream via the existing blocker flow) — the reusable 'rules as nodes' foundation for the data-quality Datalog line. First rule = the integrity gate in data-quality.rkt: current record count (live DuckDB) vs previous build's (history) blocks publish on a swing beyond threshold (default 50%). row count(*) now observed per relation (relation-row-count + '.*' observation part). Wired into beeatlas: inat-obs-integrity gate guards inat_obs_data before dbt-build. Validated end-to-end vs real beeatlas.duckdb (28850 rows: +3% pass, +188%/-52% block). 363 tests pass. NOTE: the integrity rule's logic is arithmetic (Datalog has no arithmetic) — the rule-NODE mechanism is the Datalog-enabling investment; the first genuinely relational/Datalog rule-sets are the editorial checks (st-8v3, deferred). Unreadable current count passes-with-warning (degrade, don't halt) — a policy choice worth revisiting.

H1 persistence delivered: history.rkt observation store + graph snapshots + --history + Datalog projection (first slice), plus per-key (st-6dv) and per-column (st-7vz) granularity. Deferred remainder is separate horizons: delta propagation = st-066 (H2), SQLite index (defer until a query outgrows in-memory Datalog), refs/branches/monotonic-clock (H3). See ADR 0005.

Per-key delta reasoning slice complete: pure core (delta.rkt) + impure --why/--explain adapter (delta-explain.rkt), retrospective --history consolidated onto the shared core, 406 tests green. Delta MECHANISM (targeted execution) remains under st-066.

Notes-store per-canonical_name observability complete: A1 group-digest (notes-digest.rkt) + A2 boundary-snapshot substrate (input-key-hashes on the trace, store resolver, history union). Verified end-to-end on the real store — edit one note, --why names the species. 418 tests green.

Unified the kind->per-key-layer dispatch into cache.rkt artifact-key-parts; live-key-map, observe-output (parts), and input-store-snapshot all route through it. A new keyed kind is now one case. Behaviour-preserving, 418 green.

Fixed by 9d3566e: added ecdysis_data as a checklist input so canonical_name materialization runs after the ecdysis replace-load. Validated on maderas clean cold build (species-export exit 0, species_hosts.json 541 species). Fix pushed and pulled to maderas.

site task shipped: 11ty render is a Stelis graph node (24 inputs: 9 data artifacts + 15 scoped site-src leaves), output at EXPORT_DIR/site; beeatlas _data/scripts readers honor EXPORT_DIR via lib/build-data-dir.js (public/data fallback intact). Verified: 450/450 stelis tests; beeatlas vitest unchanged (17 pre-existing SW failures on clean tree too); --run site end-to-end green (full npm run build, 1668 pages, ~13s local); --commands site plans 29 tasks/7 pruned. Early-cutoff skip + maderas timing to be observed in Phase B (st-bgy).

Serving cutover complete + verified over HTTPS. Apache vhost on maderas (infra/maderas/beeatlas.net.conf) serves /var/www/beeatlas.net; nightly.sh publishes into SITE_ROOT (merge-swap, hashed data + manifest-last) with S3/CloudFront/GH-dispatch behind PUBLISH_S3=1. DNS flipped via CDK (infra/lib/beeatlas-stack.ts, apex+www dual-stack A/AAAA to maderas; NOT a manual route53 edit — infra/README.md added). certbot cert issued (expires 2026-10-15), HTTP->HTTPS 301. Verified live: burned-in notes render no-JS, cache headers correct. Remaining before st-vjd: soak, then PUBLISH_S3=0.

Landed on main 2026-07-17: site out of the stelis graph (stelis a2fb7da), npm run fetch-data = the stelis data build (scripts/fetch-data.sh; also grew scoped args for st-nee), pull-published renamed, dead build:data deleted (beeatlas ff108233).

Landed on main 2026-07-17 (beeatlas fae1dbd0): postbuild-data.mjs derives _site/data (6 runtime binaries hashed + stable dirs + slim manifest), manifest.ts slimmed, make-local-manifest replaced. Verified live on maderas: slim manifest + hashed artifacts serving 200 after first green nightly.

Landed on main and verified on maderas 2026-07-17: §6 migration done (htdocs+var, duckdb persistent), hand-run nightly green — first-run diff tests skipped as designed, stelis 31 ok/4 cached/0 failed, gate passed, merge-swap published in 2s, baseline snapshotted, offsite backup trap ran. Cron re-enabled; tonight's 03:00 run is the soak start.

Fixed in stelis 52c7276 (cache.rkt input-hash: digest-of-pairs over resolve-store-keys for keyed stores; regression test in cache-test.rkt; 454 pass). Deployed to maderas; the stranded bombus fervidus note re-harvested (targeted, 1 key) and verified live over HTTPS.

Live in production 2026-07-17. Write path landed (beeatlas 654b73ab), gate on (NOTE_PUBLISH_ENABLED=true in the systemd unit). The FIRST production write exposed st-28p (keyed-store WAL input-addressing) — fixed in stelis 52c7276; the note then re-harvested targeted (STELIS_REBUILD_KEYS, 1 key) and is verified burned into https://beeatlas.net/species/Bombus/fervidus/ over HTTPS. Flock contention verified on maderas (bounded wait -> exit 75). Residual ops nit tracked in handoff: Apache ProxyTimeout 300 on api.beeatlas.net (sudo) for the contended-write case.

Deployed 2026-07-17 (rainhead identity): PipelineBackupBucket live (beeatlasstack-pipelinebackupbucket28e6a602-1ujf8cth0hns). Crontab PIPELINE_BACKUP_BUCKET set to the RESOLVED name (gotcha: cdk diff prints the output as {Ref: logical-id} — the real name only exists post-deploy in the stack outputs). Pipeline-user List+Put verified from maderas (raw/taxa_cache.json seeded). Tonight's nightly trap does the first full DuckDB backup to it. Unblocks st-vjd (after soak).

(a) repoint+convert. test_dbt_diff.py now resolves exporter artifacts via $EXPORT_DIR (new EXPORT constant) instead of the retired run.py SANDBOX path. The 2 byte-compares became 5 drift-idiom tests: species.json entry-count band, canonical_name anti-join, strict entry key-set; seasonality.json species count+key drift, and a structural well-formedness check (bucket prefixes, int[12] histograms). Guard skips loudly if EXPORT_DIR is unset/==public/data rather than comparing a file to itself. Verified against a real fresh export: 5/5 pass; nightly gate goes 58p+2s -> 63p. Dropped a proposed seasonality._total == species.month_histogram coherence test: FALSE invariant (month_histogram sums ecdysis+checklist months per int_species_universe.sql; seasonality counts occurrence rows only).

Fix landed on beeatlas main in time (462afb46, 2026-07-18 14:27 PDT, 'fix(test): encode the real image-loading contract for species pages'). Soak night 2 nightly (2026-07-19 03:00) ran green: JS data gate passed 41 files / 998 tests incl. build-output.data.test.ts (52 tests), pipeline complete 10:06:27Z, published + baseline snapshotted + S3 backup uploaded.

Diagnosis corrected on both counts. (1) The CI red on main was never the 17 prime/sw failures — the red runs failed on 2-3 data-dependent files (ENOENT on gitignored public/data), fixed by beeatlas 86f82021 'fix(ci): split data-dependent JS tests out of the default run'; js-tests.yml green on every main push since (8 consecutive). (2) The '17 env failures' reproduce ONLY under the wrong Node: v26 gates the localStorage global behind --localstorage-file, so test setup's localStorage.clear() throws in sw-update/species-index/cache-state tests. Under the pinned .nvmrc Node 24.18 the identical suite is 882/882 green locally. No Cache API mock/quarantine needed. Residual (untracked): caches.open/AggregateError stderr noise still spams passing runs, and a wrong-node run silently produces these phantom failures — an engines/setup guard would prevent a repeat.

Teardown executed and verified 2026-07-19. AWS (CDK, two deploys): SiteBucket, SiteDistribution E3SAI2PQ8FN0E7 (+cache/CORS/no-cache policies, 5 OACs), GitHub OIDC provider + beeatlas-github-deployer role, pipeline user's site-bucket/invalidation grants, and 4 dead outputs — all DELETE_COMPLETE, zero failures; RedirectDistribution survives with HttpOrigin('beeatlas.net') (.com→.net 301 verified); GlobalStack dropped the stale SiteCert export. Remaining on AWS: DNS, the redirect, the two RETAIN backup buckets. App side (beeatlas 102c52b4 + 9f8e7eb0): GET /api/notes deleted (now 405; write routes stay), bee-notes reload-sees-it (publish:'live' → reload; 'pending' → banner; can_edit derived from baked byline.login; 403 → whoami re-check + baked-section restore), harvest bakes body_md (verified live on Bombus fervidus), artifacts.py pruned (pull-plan replaces baseline-pull-plan), pull-published.sh credential-free HTTPS (dry-run verified: 6/6 live artifacts), nightly.sh exits 78 without PIPELINE_BACKUP_BUCKET (verified). API restarted on maderas; two hand-run nightlies green (2nd forced notes-harvest re-run after the st-top cache gap: task code is not in the input address — filed as st-top P2). Optional manual leftover: the unmanaged beeatlas-cf-logs bucket (old CF access logs).

Slice landed 2026-07-19: recipe gains a code slot (model.rkt now owns recipe/runtime types); cache hashes named script files per-file into the input address (snapshot code-hashes, CACHE-VERSION 4) plus the resolved argv via build-env runtimes (runtime identity); 'recipe-changed names the changed file(s); missing script -> conservative inputs-unresolvable. py helper auto-derives module.py, #:code declares shared helpers. dbt code + transitive imports split to a follow-up bead.

dbt code slice landed 2026-07-20: recipe-code entries may now be DIRECTORIES, expanded per-file via tree-hashes (code-path-hashes in cache.rkt) so 'code-changed names the exact model file; dbt-build declares run.sh (covers the uvx dbt pin), dbt_project.yml, profiles.yml, models/, macros/, seeds/, tests/ (~70 files) — target/, logs/, .user.yml deliberately excluded (dbt's own outputs; hashing them would self-invalidate every build). HONEST CAVEAT: the layer is latent for dbt-build today — its five gate-token inputs make it 'inputs-unresolvable, so it always runs and the code comparison is never reached; st-ysf tracks token addressability (the actual unblock). Transitive Python imports split to st-6ga (the extractor).

Landed: (1) store-keyed keyed-by kind in fan-out-key.rkt — an IDENTITY gate (files == store keyset, both strays and gaps fail sound?; missing keys fold into orphans as 'missing:<file>', the verify-manifest precedent); keys come through the same resolve-store-keys seam the cache uses (st-2k9, notes-store-keys), never a second SQL path. 'notes declares (store-keyed 'notes-store.db "{}.json"). verify-fan-out-keys gains #:resolve-store-keys; integration test adds a notes case gated on reference notes/ + a readable store. (2) Partial-rebuild precondition tightened (exec.rkt prior-complete-build?): partial mode now requires every 'dir output's CURRENT tree digest to equal the last clean run's cache receipt — an interrupted/tampered dir falls back to a full rebuild, with a printed reason; the receipt then self-heals and partial re-engages (covered in partial-rebuild-test builds 3-4). 487 tests green. NOTE: verification still runs via the integration harness like the other keyed dirs; wiring the gate into --build itself remains future work if wanted.

Gate tokens are now content-addressable (cache.rkt token-address): a 'token input hashes to the digest of its producer gate's recorded cache entry — the recipe identity + input hashes + code hashes of the gate's last PASSING run. An unchanged upstream (and unchanged gate rule/code/runtime) yields an unchanged token, so dbt-build can finally cache-skip; anything that moves the gate moves the token, surfaced on the consumer AS the token ('input-changed (tok)). Conservative by construction: no producer / no entry / an entry without a snapshot (the gate's own inputs weren't addressable) -> #f -> the consumer stays 'inputs-unresolvable, so an unaddressed verdict propagates and never launders into a false skip. This unblocks st-0ql's dbt code-hash layer, which was latent behind the short-circuit. input-snapshot/input-hash thread cache-dir; decision+snapshot passes build-env-cache-dir. 493 tests green (6 new in cache-test covering: no-entry unresolvable, pass->skip, upstream move, gate-rule move, snapshot-less entry). Same reasoning as rule-check eval-skip (st-0vz), now applied to the gate's OUTPUT. Docs: README, CLAUDE.md, plan-test comment corrected (its old 'never addressable' claim was the thing this fixes).

Extractor slice landed 2026-07-23: src/py-imports.rkt scans a script's transitive LOCAL imports at graph-authoring time (regex line scan matching how the hand list was derived; basename-set membership rejects installed packages like notes_store and docstring prose like 'from species_maps.py (RESEARCH' / 'from the matched result'). Wired into beeatlas.rkt's py() helper: recipe code is now module.py + derived closure + optional #:code escape hatch. Verified it reproduces all 14 hand #:code lists EXACTLY and fixes the documented one-hop punt (places_maps now carries config.py via places_maps->species_maps->config). All 14 hand lists deleted; #:code kept only for imports a scan can't see (dynamic/importlib, baked-in data files). 15 new tests (py-imports-test.rkt); full suite 504 green. Design fork resolved: user chose extractor+#:code auto-fill over code-as-artifacts+edges; the latter (the issue's stated hybrid end-state) split to follow-on st-whi, for which this extractor is the prerequisite.
